plan.description: This DMP is a test draft to evaluate argos as a support service to create a data management plan.
plan.title: DMP for Module 3.2, University Course "Data Steward" University Vienna

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

description.description: online survey among SEO managers and SEO copywriters provides a deeper insight into the production conditions of SEO articles
description.label: Survey Data

descriptionTemplate.label: FWF - Austrian Science Fund
descriptionTemplate.description: Dataset Description for FWF - Austrian Science Fund

page.title: Data Characteristics: Description of the data

fieldSet.title: What kinds of data/source code will be generated or reused (type, format, volume)?
How will the research data be generated and which methods will be used?
How will you structure the data and handle versioning?
Who is the target audience?

section.title: Data Characteristics: Description of the data

referenceTypeData.label: Select one or more metadata standards from the list

fieldSet.title: Will you use metadata to describe the data?

labelData.label: URL where metadata can be found
Name of repository, database, platform that holds the data

fieldSet.title: URL/Location

referenceTypeData.label: Select a vocabulary from the list

fieldSet.title: Will your metadata use standardized vocabularies?

labelData.label: URL where the vocabularies of the data can be found
Briefly describe selected vocabularies application for your needs

fieldSet.title: URL/Description

section.description: What metadata standards (if any) will be in use and why?
section.title: Metadata standards

selectData.label: Select from the list

fieldSet.title: Will all your data be openly accessible?
Data type/ Reason/ URL
Data type/ Reason/ URL

referenceTypeData.label: Select the repository where your data will be uploaded

fieldSet.title: How will the data be made available?

labelData.label: URL where the data are stored
Name of the repository that the data are stored

fieldSet.title: URL/Name

section.title: What information is needed for the data to be findable, accessible, interoperable and re-usable (FAIR) in the future?

fieldSet.title: Will you provide persistent identifiers for your data?

selectData.label: Select

fieldSet.title: Persistent identifiers
fieldSet.description: Select a persistent identifier (PID) from the list. You may select multiple PIDs according to the types of research outputs, needs and the area of your research.

fieldSet.title: Will you provide searchable metadata for your data?
What services will you use to provide searchable metadata?

labelData.label: URL of the services providing searchable metadata
Name of the services used for providing data with searchable metadata

fieldSet.title: URL/Name
How are you planning to document this information?

section.title: How are you planning to document this information?

fieldSet.title: What quality assurance processes will you adopt?
How will the consistency and quality of data collection be controlled and documented?
What persistent identifier will be used?
Will there be data that will not be stored for long-term?
What data will not be stored for long-term?
Will there be data that will be preserved for long-term?
What data will be preserved for long-term
Will you use standardised formats for some or all of your data?
Which standardised data formats do you plan on using?

labelData.label: URL where this information can be found

fieldSet.title: Standardized formats
fieldSet.description: Is the structure of the file(s) provided in a standardized format?

fieldSet.title: Please describe the formats you plan to store your data in, including any URLs to documentation.
How will the data be stored after the project ends?

selectData.label: Select

options.label: secure with no backup and recovery
insecure with no backup or recovery

fieldSet.title: Where will the data be stored after the project ends? Is the storage sufficiently secure for the data and does the storage provide backup and recovery procedures?

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers

fieldSet.title: For how long will the data be stored?
Are there any costs that need to be covered for storage?
At what point during or after the project will the data be stored?
Are there any technical barriers to making the research data fully or partially accessible?
Are there any legal barriers to making the research data fully or partially accessible?
Who owns the data?
What licence for reuse are you planning to attach to the data?
Are there any restrictions on the re-use of the data?

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers

fieldSet.title: How long do you intend to support data reuse?

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers

fieldSet.title: When do you plan to make your data available for reuse?
Are there any ethical barriers to making the research data fully or partially accessible?

selectData.label: Select from the list of answers

options.label: Kept on secure, managed storage for limited time
Kept on insecure, unmanaged storage for limited time

fieldSet.title: If applicable, how are you planning to deal with sensitive data during and after the project?
fieldSet.description: Consider "Ethics for researchers” published by the European Commission or "The European Code of Conduct for Research Integrity".

labelData.label: Provide additional information or justification about your selection

fieldSet.title: Please describe why the data will be kept on insecure, unmanaged storage

field.textValue: Protocolls and Documentation of each data version.
all data generated from umfrage.online
Institutional research data repository of University of Innsbruck and a back up copy on institutional storage system
Online survey on umfrageonline.com, results will be downloaded for preservation as *.pdf-files, estimated size of all downloads is max. 1GB
Principal Investigator
No
Online survey provider offers integrated tools for analyzing and evaluate the survey - only these tools will be used. Output can be downloaded as *.pdf-file and so be preserved.
aftertheproject
*.pdf

fieldSet.comment: The metadata, that are used by survey online tool will not be changed.
Institutional research data repository of University of Innsbruck

field.textValue: Research data repository of University of Innsbruck
https://researchdata.uibk.ac.at/
Save every version of data on institutional storage system, which is backed up daily.
yes. DOI

fieldSet.comment: There wll be no sensitive data asked in the survey.

field.textValue: Save every version of data on institutional storage system, which is backed up daily.
Answers from surveys will not be changed. All results from surveys will be generated from online survey provider. So there will be no different versions.

fieldSet.comment: The vocabulary used by survey online tool will not be changed.
researchdata.uibk.ac.at (Research data repository of University of Innsbruck) and a back up copy on institutional fileshare system.

description.description: standard webtraffic reports from Google Analytics for two Websites downloaded
description.label: Website traffic reports

fieldSet.title: If applicable, how are you planning to deal with sensitive data during and after the project?

labelData.label: Provide additional information or justification about your selection

fieldSet.title: Please describe why the data will be kept on insecure, unmanaged storage

field.textValue: standard vocabulary for pageviews in selected time period, source of pageviews, user counts
https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/api-schema
https://analytics.google.com
https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/api-schema
csv
Protocolls and Documentation of each data version as well as the R-Studio code that will evaluate the data.
raw data as well as output results from data processing as well as the used code.
Institutional research data repository of University of Innsbruck and a backup copy on institutional storage system.
csv, 2mb
Website provider
no
download from Google Analytics, access allowed from Website owner
aftertheproject

fieldSet.comment: *.csv-files for input and output data, r-studio-code for processing the data
Google traffic reports follow a report scheme, that includes standardized metadata scheme for the reported dimensions and metrics.

field.textValue: Scientists, Website-Manager

fieldSet.comment: Instutitional research data repository of University of Innsbruck

field.textValue: researchdata.uibk.ac.at
Save every version of data on institutional storage system, which is backed up daily.
DOI

fieldSet.comment: There are no sensitive data generated, that might cover  "The European Code of Conduct for Research Integrity" or other ethical policies.

field.textValue: Save every version of data on institutional storage system, which is backed up daily.
downloaded file will be saved twice: one back up copy, which will not be transformed. Data from 2nd copy will be proceed in individual written python code.

fieldSet.comment: Google traffic reports follow a report scheme, that includes standardized metadata vocabulary for the reported dimensions and metrics.
Institutional research data repository of University of Innsbruck and a backup copy on institutional storage system.

description.description: Two XML files containing all articles from the provided websites as structured text corpora, enabling corpus linguistic and text statistical analyses.
description.label: Corpus of website Articles

field.textValue: Logs and documentation of each data version.
*.xml-corpus of website articles, rstudio-code that processes the data, all genereted output data, esp. *.csv-files
Institutional research data repository of University of Innsbruck and a back up copy on institutional storage system
scraped *.html-files will be tranformed to *.xml-files, used TEI-xml, 5 MB
Project Investigator
No
automatically scraped and transformed by r-studio code.
aftertheproject

fieldSet.comment: Documentation and code to process the data.

field.textValue: Scientists, especially linguists.

fieldSet.comment: researchdata.uibk.ac.at (Research data repository of University of Innsbruck)

field.textValue: Research data repository of University of Innsbruck
researchdata.uibk.ac.at
Save every version of data on institutional storage system, which is backed up daily.
DOU

fieldSet.comment: The website corpus does not contain any sensitive data, since it is a collection of published websites.

field.textValue: Logs and documentation of each data version.
Save every version of data on institutional storage system, which is backed up daily.

fieldSet.comment:
researchdata.uibk.ac.at (Research data repository of University of Innsbruck)
and a back up copy on institutional fileshare system.
